topics_evaluated.title: Case Study: Building a Robust File Reader

terminology_consistency.summary: All shared concepts are named identically across the three topics. No synonym drift.

verified_terms.note: 7.1 defines both the `with` statement and the term 'context manager'; 7.2 and 7.3 use `with` with the same meaning and never rename it.
7.2 draws the syntax-error/exception distinction once; 7.3 uses 'exception'/'error' consistent with that framing and does not redefine it.
Identical spelling everywhere; 7.1 names it as the deferred thread, 7.2 teaches catching it, 7.3 lists it in prerequisites.
7.1 introduces csv.DictReader (rows as dicts keyed by header, line 122); 7.3 builds on it with the same name and semantics.
Introduced fresh in 7.3 and explicitly framed as 'the writer side of the csv module from 7.1' — no drift; 7.1 had introduced the sibling csv.writer/writerow.

prerequisites_respected.summary: Every topic's declared prerequisites reference only earlier-position topics, and each matches the expected declaration. No forward references detected in prose.

per_topic.forward_reference_check: pass — 7.1 deliberately DEFERS FileNotFoundError/error-handling to 7.2 and 7.3 rather than teaching it early (Introduction + Reading make the deferral explicit).
pass — depends on 7.1 (picks up the deferred FileNotFoundError thread) and on OOP 6.1-6.3 (exception-as-class / custom exception subclassing Exception). No reference to 7.3 concepts.
pass — synthesizes 7.1 (with/csv) + 7.2 (try/except/raise); introduces no concept it does not already have as a prerequisite.

learning_arc.summary: read-text -> read/write CSV+JSON -> handle errors -> robust capstone forms a complete, gap-free arc. No concept is taught twice.

arc.stage: read/write files (text, CSV, JSON) + context managers
handle runtime failures (try/except/else/finally, hierarchy, raise, custom)
synthesize into a robust skip-and-log reader with validation and clean output

learning_arc.duplicate_teaching_check: pass — try/except is taught once in 7.2 and only APPLIED in 7.3 (7.3 states 'We are not learning new syntax'). File I/O taught once in 7.1 and applied later. No overlapping Core Concepts (section 5) between topics; nouns are complementary, not redundant.

lab_activity_alignment.summary: All six labs P5.1-P5.6 are supported by at least one topic's hands-on artifact / worked example.

coverage.evidence: 7.1 Implementation Step 1 + Hands-On Exercise (read names.txt line by line).
7.1 Implementation Step 2 (csv.reader, total a numeric column).
7.3 Implementation Move 4 (csv.DictWriter writes only valid rows to people_clean.csv); Hands-On Exercise writes products_clean.csv.
7.2 'Catching a missing file' section + Implementation Step 1 (read_count catches FileNotFoundError).
7.2 'Handling multiple exceptions' + grouped except (A, B) + Hands-On safe_divide.
7.3 full 4-move worked reader (fragile -> guard -> validate -> report/write) + Hands-On products.csv extension.

weekly_time_budget.summary: Sum of expected_delivery_minutes = 30 (10+10+10) vs a 90-minute weekly target. Not a hard fail.

findings.detail: This is a structural artifact of a 3-topic capstone week. Each reading is already at the deep-profile maximum (~10 min; reading_minutes_range [9,11] in every manifest, confirmed_profile 'deep'). Depth cannot be raised further without exceeding the profile ceiling, and the learning arc is already complete. Reported as INFO, not a coherence failure.

notes: Coherence is clean across terminology, prerequisites, learning arc, no-duplication, and lab alignment. The only finding is the soft/INFO time-budget underrun, which is expected and acceptable for a maxed-depth 3-topic capstone week.
